_source: VeraData /sanctions conformance fixture, provided by teodorofodocrispin-cmyk in x402-foundation/x402#2749 (2026-07-01T15:51:49Z)

recompute_construction.query_hash_note: name normalized (lowercased, brackets stripped) + country + lists sorted alphabetically, pipe-delimited
recompute_construction.prefix_convention: IMPORTANT (found by independent recompute, not stated explicitly in the source comment): every hash reference used as an input to a SUBSEQUENT hash includes its own literal 'sha256:' prefix. Bare hex inputs (no prefix) produce a DIFFERENT hash and fail to match the claimed event_hash/chain_hash. This is the load-bearing detail a naive recompute would silently get wrong.

expected.risk_category_overclaim_check: risk_score=0.0/CLEAN must be read as 'not found in these lists at checked_at' — never as a global clean-entity claim. matches=[] over lists_checked is the honest scope of what this fixture attests to.
